name: Claude Opus 4.6
note: First Opus-class model with a 1M-token context window in beta (Claude Developer Platform only) plus Claude Code agent teams; also exposes low/medium/high/max effort levels (reported here at default high).

results.notes: Standard-scaffold figure (Anthropic's Feb 2026 system card reports 80.84%, averaged over 25 trials, max effort/adaptive thinking); Anthropic's own launch post cites a separate 81.42% obtained 'with a prompt modification' — a different methodology, not this headline number.

Terminal-Bench 2.0; per Anthropic's Feb 2026 system card this figure was measured at max effort with adaptive thinking, not the model's 'default high' setting — corrected from the original 'default (high) effort' label.

verificationNotes: Confirmed real and current (today is 2026-07-12): anthropic.com/news/claude-opus-4-6 and Anthropic's Feb 2026 system card verify the 2026-02-05 release date. Corrected the note's claim of '1M-token context at GA' to 'in beta' — Anthropic's own page states the 1M window is 'in beta on the Claude Developer Platform only,' not GA. GPQA (91.3), HLE-without-tools (40.0), and MRCR-v2 1M (76%) were confirmed as literal text on their cited pages. Swapped the sourceUrl for the swe-bench and terminal-bench-2 rows away from anthropic.com (whose blog shows 81.42% for SWE-bench and no literal terminal-bench percentage) to vellum.ai's benchmark breakdown, which literally states both 80.8% and 65.4%. Also corrected the terminal-bench-2 row's effort-level description: per the system card the 65.4% figure was measured at max effort with adaptive thinking, not 'default (high) effort' as originally labeled.
